title: Charm unlocks closed time anchor
description: A shrine charm (or equivalent seal) provides the anchor match required to enable non-destructive ritual+tech stabilisation mechanics for the local time anomaly.

fallbacks.note: If S06 or S07 is mirrored to Chronicle by chapter 3, they satisfy S05's role for P02 canonical mechanics.

data.requirements_description: Requires one mirrored shrine-seal seed (S05 preferred) to enable ritual+tech non-destructive procedure.

notes: At least one of S05 or S06 must be mirrored to Chronicle to enable canonical mechanics; if neither mirrored by latest_chapter, present cinematic alternative but do NOT enable canonical mechanic.

title: Chronos Division exposure (corporate front)
description: Evidence showing Chronos Division is a corporate front for Sable Group. Allows canonical exposure mechanics/quests later.

fallbacks.note: Security footage / manifests can substitute if mirrored by chapter 10.

data.gameplay: opens public exposure questline; unlocks new NPC interactions and evidence-collection mechanics

notes: At least one of the listed seeds must be mirrored to Chronicle before latest_chapter to get canonical exposure mechanics. Otherwise allow restricted cinematic reveal that advances plot but doesn't unlock investigative mechanics.

title: Stabilization protocol (ritual + tech synergy)
description: A multi-team stabilization procedure combining charm (ritual) and pulse (tech) to non-destructively stabilise anchors. Canonical mechanics require documentary proof of the protocol or Engine Memory Core evidence.

fallbacks.note: If S16 is missed, Kenta's notebook or training clip may be mirrored by chapter 11 to permit canonical mechanics.

data.requires: at least one mirrored seed listed above
data.fallback_behavior: if missing, present cinematic rescue that resolves plot but disables non-destructive mechanic unlock

notes: S16 or S23 must be mirrored to Chronicle to allow canonical P05 mechanics; if neither is mirrored by latest_chapter, only cinematic resolution occurs.
